summary: Tested CarDost Vehicle Catalog v2 (hierarchical Make→Model→Variant). Backend: 18/18 new tests PASS in test_cardost_vehicles.py covering catalog tree, make/model/variant CRUD + cascade delete, variant-label endpoint, product compatibility persistence, /products/filter by variant_id/model_id/make_id with Universal union, legacy car_brand/car_model/year filters, order persistence of vehicle_variant_id + vehicle_label. Frontend Playwright verified: 9 admin tabs render, tab-vehicles shows all 9 makes, Honda→Amaze expands to '1st Generation' (2013) and '2nd Generation' pills, product Add dialog hosts vehicle-picker-search + cb-ALL toggle, ALL toggle hides picker and shows 'Universal product' banner, picker search 'Creta' filters tree to Hyundai.
action_items: Optional: customer CustomerVehicleSelector could not be functionally exercised against existing seed products because none of the 19 seeded products have compatible_variants populated (their legacy car_brands array is empty too). The selector logic correctly suppresses itself in this case. To smoke-test the customer flow end-to-end, the main agent (or a future test) can either (a) seed one product with a real compatible_variants entry, or (b) create one via the admin UI; backend integration is already proven via pytest.
Carry-over from iteration 2 (not retested): rename data-testid 'tab-settings' → 'tab-integrations' for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: filter_products correctly resolves model_id and make_id via DB sub-queries to variant IDs and unions with Universal (car_brands='ALL') — verified by 6 dedicated tests.
DELETE /admin/catalog/makes/{mid} cascades to models then variants in correct order — verified.
Order create endpoint persists vehicle_variant_id (Optional[str]) and vehicle_label ('' default) verbatim on each item_detail — verified.
CartItem model in server.py correctly defaults vehicle_variant_id=None and vehicle_label='' so legacy callers without these fields still work.
/catalog/tree response groups variants by model_id and models by make_id efficiently in O(N) — good.
Note: server.py line 30-33 reads RAZORPAY_KEY_ID via os.environ[...] which will crash on startup if missing; consider .get with sensible fallback for resilience.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 new + 16/16 universal + 22/24 legacy [2 transient network timeouts unrelated to vehicles])

seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_VehFilterProduct, TEST_UniversalProduct, TEST_LegacyProduct plus a TESTBrand make/model/variant tree. All cleaned via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three backend test files in /app/backend/tests/: test_cardost_api.py, test_cardost_universal.py, test_cardost_vehicles.py. All run via 'REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://stereo-connect-2.preview.emergentagent.com pytest backend/tests/'. Note: legacy test_cardost_api.py + test_cardost_universal.py read REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL via os.environ[...] without default — they fail collection if env not set. test_cardost_vehicles.py uses .get(...) fallback. Vehicle catalog data is idempotently seeded from /app/backend/data/car_database.json on startup (see seed_vehicle_catalog). The admin Vehicles tab and VehicleVariantPicker are functional. Customer CustomerVehicleSelector only renders for products with compatible_variants or car_brands containing 'ALL'; the 19 seeded products have neither, which is acceptable legacy behavior.
